title: Variants passing four simultaneous conditions
description: Apply four filters jointly: FILTER=PASS AND per-sample DP>=30 AND genotype is heterozygous (0/1) AND chromosome is NOT chr3. Count survivors. Tests compositional filter logic with one unusual constraint (chromosome exclusion).
task_prompt: You are given a VCF file at sample.vcf in your working directory. Apply ALL of the following four conditions and count how many variant records survive: (1) the FILTER column (column 7) is exactly 'PASS', (2) the per-sample read depth (DP subfield of the SAMPLE1 column at the position indicated by the FORMAT column) is >= 30, (3) the genotype (GT subfield) is exactly '0/1' (heterozygous), AND (4) the chromosome (column 1, CHROM) is NOT 'chr3'. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with this exact schema: {"n_passing": <integer>}.
notes: Walking through the 10 variants: PASS rows are chr1:1000, chr1:3000, chr2:1500, chr2:2500, chr3:5000, chr3:7000. Of those: chr1:3000 is 1/1 (hom, fails GT); chr2:2500 has DP=22<30 (fails DP); chr3:5000 and chr3:7000 are chr3 (fails NOT-chr3) and also 1/1. Survivors: chr1:1000 (PASS, DP=30, 0/1, chr1) and chr2:1500 (PASS, DP=60, 0/1, chr2). Expected = 2. Authoring note: an earlier draft of this eval had expected_answer=3; Claude correctly returned 2 and the grader flagged the mismatch. Documented as a methodology lesson - hand-crafted ground truth is error-prone; future bulk-generated evals should derive expected answers programmatically.
